Literal.Value:
'import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.ticker import FuncFormatter

# Professional color palette
COLORS = {
    "primary": "#5B8DBE",
    "gray_700": "#495057",
    "gray_600": "#6C757D",
    "gray_400": "#ADB5BD"
}

# Month order for sorting
month_order = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"]

# Create month factor and sort
dataset["month_index"] = dataset["Calendar Month (ie Jan)"].apply(lambda x: month_order.index(x) if x in month_order else -1)
dataset = dataset.sort_values("month_index")

# Create figure
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8.3, 2.3))

# Fill area under line
ax.fill_between(
    dataset["month_index"],
    0,
    dataset["Order Lines"],
    color=COLORS["primary"],
    alpha=0.12
)

# Line plot
ax.plot(
    dataset["month_index"],
    dataset["Order Lines"],
    color=COLORS["primary"],
    linewidth=2.4,
    marker="o",
    markerfacecolor="white",
    markeredgecolor=COLORS["primary"],
    markersize=6,
    markeredgewidth=3
)

# X-axis: month labels
ax.set_xticks(range(12))
ax.set_xticklabels(month_order)

# Y-axis: K format
def format_k(x, pos):
    return f"{int(round(x/1000))}K"

ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(FuncFormatter(format_k))

# Grid and spines
ax.grid(axis="y", color=COLORS["gray_400"], linewidth=0.6, alpha=0.5)
ax.grid(axis="x", visible=False)
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["left"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_visible(False)

# Labels
ax.set_ylabel("Order Lines", fontsize=9, color=COLORS["gray_700"])
ax.set_xlabel("")

# Tick styling
ax.tick_params(
    axis="both",
    labelsize=8,
    colors=COLORS["gray_600"],
    length=0
)

# Set y-axis to start at 0
ax.set_ylim(bottom=0)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()'
